basics.name: Edgar Aroutiounian
basics.label: Software engineer
basics.summary: Polyglot, fast learner, generalist software developer with special strength in functional programming. Broad experience with mixing languages like JavaScript and OCaml, OCaml and Objective-C/C++. Driven by creative solutions, ideas. I love open-source and building things that ship. Lately been focused on ReactNative powered mobile development, I also emphasize teaching and made a hackerspace in Armenia

work.summary: Doing any kind of coding for the needs of expo
highlights: ReactNative infrastructure
Android JNI work

work.summary: Mainly ReactNative development for paying clients, implemented Objective-C solutions when necessary as ReactNative occasionally did not expose proper APIs.
highlights: Product Development
Client management
Clients included IBM Russia and various startups

work.summary: In charge of entire iOS downloading pipeline including custom jailbreak software for iOS written in Objective-C++, cydia substrate. Created an open-source project, ocaml-usbmux, for allowing sshing of iPhones from either OS X or Linux.
highlights: Created an OCaml open source project, ocaml-usbmux
Released a nixpkgs for an iOS cross compiler on Linux
Wrote jailbreak solutions used in production
Increased downloading throughout by 150%

work.summary: Worked on frontend of a large scale webcrawler implemented in OCaml. Used bindings to Google's DFA based RE2 regex engine for performance in assigned task; cluster reindexed the entire reachable web in 28 days.
highlights: Analyzed what web technologies powered a website in one pass and on average in 0.003 seconds
Initial move to the Bay Area from New York City

work.summary: Worked on a part time basis as a contractor.
highlights: Started an OCaml library for Amazon Web Services
Was mentored weekly in OCaml type theory/system
Helped with edits and minor fixes to Real World OCaml

work.summary: Worked on a internal Line of Business application for Liquidity Risk reporting.
highlights: Exposed new C# GUI widgets to existing Python 2.6 for .NET usage
Utilizied internal graph library with Python Classes that reactively updated GUI
Helped distributed team of former Java programmers acclimate to Python

volunteer.summary: I made the first hackerspace in Yerevan, Armenia. Taught programming for free to many students, mentored and helped start the careers of many talented young people.
highlights: Mentored many young people

volunteer.organization: OCaml meetup group of Silicon Valley
volunteer.summary: I run the meetup, organize events and speakers. I also hold regular office hours on Fridays providing any kind of OCaml needed help
highlights: Hosted a talk by Matthew Saffer from Jane Street
Hold weekly office hours on Fridays
Presented several talks introducing functional programming
